methods.description: Process for cultivating Phaeodactylum tricornutum in controlled conditions for experimental analysis.

steps.description: Phaeodactylum tricornutum was obtained from the Algal Collection Center of Jinan University (no. FACHB-863).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain Phaeodactylum tricornutum from Jinan University collection
inputs: Algal Collection Center of Jinan University

steps.description: Cells were grown as batch cultures in flasks containing f/2-Si medium (omitting Na 2 SiO 3 ·9H 2 O).
steps.searchable_summary: grow cells in f/2-Si medium without Na2SiO3

steps.description: Cultures were routinely cultivated in an artificial climate incubator under constant irradiance (200 μmol photons m −2 s −1 ), temperature (21±0.5°C), and 12/12-h light/dark cycles.
steps.searchable_summary: cultivate cultures in controlled climate conditions

methods.method_name: Algal Culture Treatment for Fatty Acid Profiling
methods.description: Preparation of algal cultures for fatty acid profiling and 2D analysis by manipulating nitrogen levels.

steps.description: Algal culture (1.5 L) in the late logarithmic growth phase was centrifuged (4,400 rpm, 10 min, 4°C) and the pelleted cells were washed twice with N-free f/2-Si medium.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge and wash algal culture with N-free medium

steps.description: Cells were collected by centrifugation (4,400 rpm, 15 min), and pelleted cells were reinoculated into 1.5 L of N-free f/2-Si medium.
steps.searchable_summary: re-inoculate cells into N-free medium

steps.description: Half of the cultures were supplemented with N. The resuspended cultures were evenly divided into 12 250-mL flasks and cultures supplemented with N as control.
steps.searchable_summary: divide cultures and supplement with nitrogen

steps.description: Cells were cultured for a further 2 days for fatty acid profiling or 2D analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: culture cells for fatty acid profiling

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing protein expression using image analysis and mass spectrometry.

steps.description: Images of silver-stained gels acquired with an image scanner (Amersham Biosciences, USA) were analyzed using ImageMaster2D Platinum 6.0 (GE, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze gel images using ImageMaster2D

steps.description: Spots with statistical significance (Student's t test, p<0.05) were considered, and spots with an abundance ratio of at least two were selected as differentially expressed proteins.
steps.searchable_summary: select significant protein spots

steps.description: Differentially expressed spots were manually excised from the gel.
steps.searchable_summary: excise protein spots from gel

steps.description: In-gel digestion and mass spectrometry analysis were performed according to Zhao et al. (2005).
steps.searchable_summary: perform in-gel digestion and mass spectrometry

steps.description: The identified proteins were then matched to specific processes or functions by querying Gene Ontology (http://www.geneontology.org/).
steps.searchable_summary: match proteins to functions using Gene Ontology

methods.description: Measurement of chlorophyll fluorescence to assess photosynthetic performance in P. tricornutum.

steps.description: The chlorophyll fluorescence in P. tricornutum culture was measured with a Handy PEA chlorophyll fluorometer (Hansatech Instruments Ltd., UK).
steps.searchable_summary: measure chlorophyll fluorescence using fluorometer

steps.description: Algae culture was kept in the dark for 20 min prior to exposure to saturation pulse light (3,000 mol photons m −2 s −1 ) for 1 s, followed by measurement of F v /F m (the ratio of variable/maximum fluorescence).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare algae culture for fluorescence measurement

methods.description: Determination of fatty acid composition in diatom cultures using gas chromatography-mass spectrometry.

steps.description: A 250-mL aliquot of diatom culture was harvested 48 h after N deprivation (−N).
steps.searchable_summary: harvest diatom culture after N deprivation

steps.description: Lipids were extracted according to Lepage, with minor modifications (Liu and Curtiss 2009).
steps.searchable_summary: extract lipids from culture

steps.description: Fatty acid composition was determined by gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS) at the Institute of Microbiology of Guangdong, China.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze fatty acids using GC-MS

steps.description: A DB-5 quartz capillary column with specifications 30 m×0.25 mm×0.25 μm was used. The column temperature was programmed from 60 to 160°C at a rate of 10°C min −1 and to a final temperature of 250°C at a rate of 2.5°C min −1.
steps.searchable_summary: set up GC-MS column temperature program

steps.description: Injector was set at 280°C and splitless was 1 μl. Mass spectrum transmission line was set at 200°C.
steps.searchable_summary: configure GC-MS injector and transmission line

steps.description: Fatty acid composition was identified with the equipped NBS spectrum library. Peak areas were calculated by normalization to acquire the percent contents.
steps.searchable_summary: identify fatty acids using NBS spectrum library

methods.method_name: Protein Expression Analysis in Genetic Information Processing
methods.description: Analysis of protein expression changes in response to nitrogen deficiency, focusing on heat shock proteins and elongation factors.

steps.description: We identified similar trends in N-deprived cells, including two HSPs, which were dramatically upregulated: Hsp70 (PHATRDRAFT_41417) and Hsp90 (PHATR DRAFT_55215).
steps.searchable_summary: identify upregulated HSPs in N-deprived cells

steps.description: A putative elongation factor G (EF-G; PHATRDRAFT_ 11189) showed higher expression following −N.
steps.searchable_summary: identify upregulated EF-G in N-deprived cells

steps.description: A slight decrease was observed for another protein involved in genetic information processing, the polyadenylate binding protein (PHATRDRAFT_23959).
steps.searchable_summary: identify decreased expression of polyadenylate binding protein

methods.method_name: Mapping Protein Expression Data to KEGG Pathways
methods.description: Mapping identified proteins to KEGG pathways to study metabolic changes in response to nitrogen deprivation.

steps.description: All proteins identified in the 2D proteomic analysis were mapped to the Kyoto Encyclopedia of Genes and Genomes (KEGG) pathway IDs using both the KEGG database and homology mapping.
steps.searchable_summary: map proteins to KEGG pathways

steps.description: Direct neighbors and second degree interactions that could connect two identified proteins were included to construct the metabolic connections.
steps.searchable_summary: construct metabolic connections from protein interactions

steps.description: Expression level changes were represented by color intensity.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize expression changes with color intensity
